data.title: FactSet disappoints with Q4 results, shares down 8.8%. Q4 revenue: $596.9M, EPS: $4.05. Full year revenue: $2.32B, EPS: $16.98. 2026 guidance below Wall Street estimates, raises concerns about margins and ASV growth. Market reacts quickly, stock among S&P 500 decliners.
data.description: FactSet reported fourth-quarter results below expectations and provided fiscal 2026 guidance that fell short of Wall Street estimates. Revenue for the quarter was $596.9 million, up 6.2% from the previous year, with adjusted EPS at $4.05, an 8.3% increase. Full-year revenue reached $2.32 billion, a 5.4% rise, with adjusted EPS at $16.98, up 3.2%. However, management's guidance for fiscal 2026 indicated lower revenue expectations, with GAAP revenue projected between $2.423 and $2.448 billion, organic ASV growth at 4% to 6%, and adjusted EPS between $16.90 and $17.60. This outlook sparked concerns about margin trajectory and ASV growth, leading to an 8.8% drop in FactSet's stock price as investors reassessed growth and profitability expectations. The company's ability to improve renewal pricing, capitalize on GenAI products, and control expenses will be critical for its future performance amidst competitive pressures from Bloomberg and Refinitiv.

data.title: Price prediction for WLD aims for $2.20 breakout in 2 weeks, signaling a 44% potential upside. Bearish scenarios suggest $1.40 retest if support fails.
data.description: According to recent analysis, there is a potential 44% increase in the WLD price, which could reach a resistance level of $2.20 in the next two weeks. However, if the current support level does not hold, bearish outcomes indicate a possible retest at $1.40.

data.title: "Tour guide app Le Walk raises $4.1M in seed funding for global expansion and new features by 2026"
data.description: Le Walk, a prominent location-based guided experiences app, has announced a plan for global expansion, feature enhancements, and the introduction of new premium offerings by 2026. This initiative is being supported by top venture capitalists, with a $4.1 million seed funding round led by Adverb. The company made its public debut in New York on September 19, 2025.
